name: Mushroom Almond Pate
recipeInstructions:
1. Spread almonds in a shallow pan and toast in a 350°F oven for 8 minutes or until lightly browned.
2. Melt butter in a large frying pan over medium-high heat. Add onion, garlic, mushrooms, salt, thyme, and pepper. Cook, stirring occasionally, until onion is soft and most of the pan juices have evaporated.
3. In a food processor or blender, whirl almonds to form a paste. With the motor running, add oil and whirl until creamy.
4. Add mushroom mixture and whirl until pate is smooth.
5. Chill until set.
I used walnuts because I had them on hand, and this turned out fine. Served it alongside thinly sliced toasted rounds of Italian bread. Yum. Next to that, I had a wedge of brie surrounded by bite-sized hunks of carrots (cutting veggies to bite size saves the worry some people have about others who dip, bite, then stick something bitten back into the dip). And a dip for veggies I make, which is yummy but not for the faint of heart.
1. Combine 1 cup nonfat yogurt with 1½ tbsp apricot honey (or 1 tbsp honey plus ½ tbsp apricot preserves) in a bowl.
2. Add 1 tsp curry powder and ½ tsp cayenne pepper; stir until well combined.
3. Let sit for at least an hour to allow flavors to intensify, but you may want to add more curry or cayenne later. The dip tastes sweet with a hint of heat that becomes noticeable only after consumption.
